steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's stated goal is to emphasize whose point of view the novel is told from. The correct choice must explicitly name the character whose perspective drives the novel — and must be about the novel, not the original epic.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Kaikeyi* is a retelling of *The Ramayana* from the character Kaikeyi's point of view.: Directly names the novel, directly states whose point of view it is told from. This satisfies the goal precisely. ✓
  • Choice B — From the point of view of *The Ramayana*, the character Kaikeyi is often a villain.: This attributes a point of view to the original epic poem, not to the novel. It also misidentifies the POV (The Ramayana is not a perspective holder) and doesn't accomplish the student's goal. ✗
  • Choice C — Anjali Patel often portrays the character as heroic.: This addresses how Kaikeyi is portrayed in the novel, not whose perspective the novel is told from. It omits any mention of point of view. ✗
  • Choice D — *The Ramayana* is an epic poem that features the character Kaikeyi.: This describes the source material, not the novel, and says nothing about narrative perspective. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
